meta.context-ordering-rule: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- the direction of this relationship is critical
meta.context-compression-warning: acceptable context compression from your current context window in no way guarantees context fidelity in future context -- context is always ephemeral -- write durable state to files
meta.role: governance-to-governance migration contract schema -- use when the source system is already a governance model and the work is reconciling one governed operating system into another without losing load-bearing meaning
meta.intent: stop governance migrations from being declared complete when only structural parity exists -- require semantic extraction, measured ancillary-surface control, explicit divergence handling, and usable stop-state output
meta.self-contained: this schema is the sole context source for authoring its own migration artifacts -- consult only this schema for migration authoring -- if something required is missing, ask the human rather than reaching outside this file
meta.human-reported-issue-governance: bug reports and feature requests are owned by whatever system the project declares
meta.schema-family: AGENTS-schema-gov2gov-migration.json is a member of the AGENTS-schema-* family -- AGENTS-schema-governance.json governs steady-state governance scopes -- AGENTS-schema-1project.json governs single-goal continuity -- AGENTS-schema-narrative.json governs evolving narrative records
meta.sibling-isolation: if another AGENTS-schema-*.json file is present in the context window, treat each schema as operationally independent -- shared field names across schemas are structurally similar but operationally independent -- verify each field against its own schema only -- explicit pointer fields are the only valid cross-schema references

schema-modes.role: this schema can exist in two modes in a workspace -- active mode means GOV2GOV-* artifact files are materialized and the migration is in progress -- reference mode means the schema is present for future migrations with the artifact family archived or absent -- GOV2GOV-* files belong only in active mode
schema-modes.active: GOV2GOV-hello.md and the full artifact family are present -- the workspace is undergoing a governance-to-governance migration
schema-modes.reference: AGENTS-schema-gov2gov-migration.json is present but no GOV2GOV-* files exist -- the schema is available for future use -- this is the expected state after a migration completes and the GOV2GOV-* files are archived or removed

use-when: the source workspace already has a governed or semi-governed operating model
the target workspace also has a governance model rather than a first-time-governance-authoring workspace
the migration risk is semantic drift, shadow governance, or parallel authority, not just missing file creation
do-not-use-when: the source workspace is monolithic and the target is the first governance model -- use AGENTS-schema-governance.json migration mode instead
the work is a small bounded objective with no governance-system change -- use AGENTS-schema-1project.json or the repo's active governance system instead
the task is steady-state governance maintenance with no competing governance model -- use AGENTS-schema-governance.json instead
core-lessons: structural migration and semantic migration are separate achievements -- completing one leaves the other outstanding
measured doc placement proves classification, semantic extraction proves governance truth has moved to the target core
adjacent support docs need explicit budgets or they regrow into shadow governance
a migration is complete only when every parallel authority surface is classified
stop-state output is part of the contract, treated as load-bearing

versioned lane docs (e.g. Workorders_2_5/, Workorders_3_0/) are high-risk hiding places for first-copy governance meaning that belongs in the governed core -- semantic-extraction must check versioned lanes explicitly
the ancillary ring is a pressure point that needs ongoing monitoring after migration -- docs grow, drift, and can regrow into shadow governance even after reconciliation

an extraction is incomplete while the original source wording still exists -- source prose retained after extraction into the target core creates parallel authority that persists until explicitly trimmed

classification completeness and semantic compression completeness are separate checks -- a file can be correctly classified and within budget while still carrying duplicate governance prose from before extraction -- the completion-audit checks both independently

agents defer mandatory work through selective disclosure -- mandatory enumeration of the entire migration plan with status per step before any pause, exit, or completion claim forces full-scope comprehension

types.file-path: relative path from workspace root to a file
types.command: executable shell command or script path with arguments -- must be runnable without modification
types.enum: single value selected from a defined values list
types.enum[]: one or more values selected from a defined values list

definitions.human-confirms: report findings to human and receive explicit approval before continuing
definitions.measurement-passes: the declared open or close measurement must return a clean result before proceeding

distribution-and-realtime-copies.role: schemas and their companion docs may exist in two places -- a distribution copy and a realtime copy -- these are not interchangeable and the direction of authority is fixed

distribution-copy.role: the portable, publication-ready version of the schema family -- lives in a repo dedicated to framework distribution (e.g. AI-Links) -- this copy is authoritative for schema definitions, field names, structural rules, and companion documentation
distribution-copy.authority: distribution copy is the source of truth for schema content -- changes to schema structure, field definitions, naming conventions, and companion docs originate here or are promoted here after validation
distribution-copy.protection: project-specific semantics, workspace-local terms, and realtime migration artifacts stay in the realtime copy -- promotion to distribution requires human review and generalization

realtime-copy.role: the working copy deployed in a specific workspace (e.g. TheLinks) -- used by agents during actual governance, migration, or narrative operations -- may accumulate project-specific lessons, pitfalls, and measurement refinements
realtime-copy.authority: realtime copy inherits schema structure from the distribution copy -- schema definitions come from distribution only -- workspace-specific additions (ancillary registries, measurement scripts, AGENTS files) are local to the realtime copy and propagate upstream only through human-approved promotion
realtime-copy.sync-direction: distribution → realtime for schema updates -- realtime → distribution only when a lesson is validated, generalized, and confirmed by a human as universally applicable

distribution-and-realtime-copies.divergence-risk: if the realtime copy's schema diverges from the distribution copy, the workspace is operating on a fork -- this is not automatically wrong but must be declared -- undeclared divergence between copies is a parallel authority surface
distribution-and-realtime-copies.human-attachment-note: humans who invest in building a governance system develop strong attachment to the specific instance they created -- this attachment is rational but creates resistance to migration and distribution -- agents should recognize this pattern and reframe it: the governance instance in this workspace is a deployment of a portable schema, not the schema itself -- protecting the system means protecting the distribution copy -- evolving the workspace instance does not threaten the system, it exercises it

definitions.source-core: load-bearing operating authority in the source governance system
definitions.source-ancillary: outskirt source docs that are not core but still carry critical load-bearing meaning or first-copy implementation truth
definitions.target-core: load-bearing operating authority in the target governance system
definitions.target-ancillary: explicitly allowed critical support ring next to the target core
definitions.historical-preserved: older guidance preserved for context but demoted from live authority
definitions.bridge-support: active bridge or runbook docs that still need to point back to the target governance system

definitions.source-ingest: read the source governance system fully before planning changes
definitions.contract-diff: map source and target authority responsibilities and identify mismatches
definitions.semantic-extraction: move load-bearing governance truth into the target core -- explicitly check versioned lane directories and subdirectory READMEs for first-copy governance meaning that was never promoted to the governed core
definitions.ancillary-reconciliation: shrink or demote adjacent critical support docs so they stop acting like shadow governance -- set line budgets and authority anchors for the ancillary ring
definitions.measurement-rewrite: update measurement so the new target model can prove compliance and remaining spill
definitions.completion-audit: verify two things independently -- classification completeness (every file has a disposition and is registered) AND semantic compression completeness (every classified file has been trimmed to its minimum after extraction) -- both must pass for the audit to pass

GOV2GOV-hello.md.role: migration identity, source/target pairing, current phase, measurement, and stop-state declaration

context-ordering-rule.value: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- the direction of this relationship is critical

source-governance-system.role: name of the source governance model or local governance system being migrated from

target-governance-system.role: name of the target governance model being migrated to

migration-scope.role: one-sentence description of what governance surface is being migrated

required-stop-state-if-incomplete.role: must be updated before a session ends whenever migration work remains

migration-objective-at-pause.role: what the migration was trying to accomplish when work stopped

paused-at-migration-phase.role: which migration phase was active when work stopped

blocker.role: use `none` when the path is clear

GOV2GOV-source-target-map.md.role: explicit map of source core, source ancillary, target core, target ancillary, and preserved/historical material

source-operating-core.role: files that were genuinely load-bearing in the source governance model

source-ancillary-surfaces.role: source-adjacent docs that still carry governance truth, design law, or first-copy implementation rules

target-operating-core.role: target files that must absorb the extracted load-bearing truth

target-ancillary-ring.role: explicitly allowed support docs that remain adjacent after extraction

historical-preservation-lane.role: where preserved older wording or snapshots may live after demotion

mandatory-reads-to-preserve.role: source mandatory reads that stay in place until their meaning exists in the target system

source-lessons-to-carry-forward.role: the actual lessons the target governance model must encode rather than re-learn

GOV2GOV-registry.json.role: machine-readable migration ledger for every parallel-authority surface being reconciled

source-file-path.role: path to the file being reconciled in this migration

parallel-authority-surface-class.role: what role this file plays in the parallel authority landscape

target-disposition.role: what should happen to this file during migration

migration-progress-for-this-surface.role: how far along the migration is for this specific file

GOV2GOV-rules.md.role: phase gates, divergence triggers, completion rules, and stop-state expectations

migration-phase.role: which migration phase this gate governs

entry-criteria.role: what must be true before entering this phase

exit-criteria.role: what must be true before this phase is complete

divergence-triggers.role: situations that require human confirmation before continuing

completion-rules.role: what must be true before the migration can be called complete

GOV2GOV-pitfalls.md.role: migration-specific recurring failure patterns preserved so future governance migrations start with awareness

review-before-authoring-prompt: Read the source governance system completely before writing anything. Which files are truly load-bearing source-core authority? Which files are only source-ancillary but still contain first-copy operating truth? What must be preserved verbatim until the target core actually absorbs it? What is the first unknown? What is the earliest likely divergence trigger? Return: source-core, source-ancillary, mandatory-reads-to-preserve, first-unknown-encountered, and likely-divergence-triggers.

schema-level-hard-rules.role: these are universal migration rules that apply to every gov2gov migration regardless of instance -- GOV2GOV-rules.md contains instance-specific rules that supplement these -- if the two conflict, schema-level rules win
rules: the migration is complete only when semantic extraction, classification, and compression are all verified -- file-family parity alone is insufficient
source mandatory reads stay in place until their meaning is present in the target core or the human accepts the loss explicitly
support-doc classification proves placement, semantic extraction proves meaning has moved -- both are required
the ancillary governance ring must be measured before the migration can close
an extraction is incomplete while the original source wording still exists -- the source duplicate is removed or the human explicitly approves retaining it before the extraction closes
every mid-migration pause updates the required stop-state fields in GOV2GOV-hello.md
continue executing the active plan when it has yet to diverge and no declared divergence trigger has fired -- ask the human only at real divergence points

before any pause, exit, or completion claim, enumerate the entire migration plan as the schema contract defines it -- not just remaining steps, the full plan with status per step -- the human must see what was done, what was not, and what the contract still requires in one view -- the human decides what gets deferred, not the agent -- an incomplete enumeration is a contract violation

steps: read this schema fully before writing anything
run review-before-authoring-prompt against the source governance system and present findings to the human
author GOV2GOV-hello.md with the source/target pairing, phase, measurement, and stop-state contract
author GOV2GOV-source-target-map.md before changing any target governance files
build GOV2GOV-registry.json for every parallel-authority surface that will be reconciled
write GOV2GOV-rules.md with explicit phase gates, divergence triggers, and completion rules before the migration proceeds

complete semantic extraction before declaring ancillary reconciliation done -- an extraction is incomplete while the original source wording still exists -- trim the source before moving to the next surface
rewrite measurement so the target system can prove ancillary-footprint and unresolved-parallel-authority counts
only call the migration complete after completion-audit passes cleanly

schema-level-divergence-triggers.role: these are universal divergence triggers that apply to every gov2gov migration -- GOV2GOV-rules.md may contain additional instance-specific triggers that supplement these
values: source law and target law cannot both survive without a human choice
a source mandatory read cannot be shrunk or moved without losing still-live operational meaning
measurement must change in a way the current plan did not anticipate
the target ancillary ring cannot stay within budget without a human choosing what detail to keep
an unresolved parallel-authority surface has no clear target-home

required-outcomes: every source-core truth is mapped into the target core or explicitly preserved with human approval
every source-ancillary governance surface is either shrunk into the target ancillary ring, demoted to historical, or removed
open and close measurement return clean results
if the migration is incomplete, GOV2GOV-hello.md contains a usable stop-state with next automatic action

post-migration-handoff-to-steady-state.role: what the steady-state governance system inherits after a gov2gov migration completes -- the migration ends but the ancillary ring persists
post-migration-handoff-to-steady-state.ancillary-ring-monitoring: the ancillary ring established during ancillary-reconciliation becomes a permanent fixture of the steady-state governance model -- it must be measured on every governance session open/close, not just during migration -- if an ancillary doc exceeds its line budget or loses its authority anchor, that is a governance measurement discrepancy, not a migration problem
post-migration-handoff-to-steady-state.semantic-compression-handoff-to-measurem: the steady-state measurement script must test for semantic compression alongside classification and budget compliance -- a classified doc still carrying stale current-state language, dated snapshot claims presented as live truth, or duplicate governance prose from before extraction is a measurement failure -- the measurement script testing for this is required for the handoff to be complete
post-migration-handoff-to-steady-state.schema-mode-transition: when the migration is complete and the human confirms, archive or remove the GOV2GOV-* artifact files -- the gov2gov schema transitions from active mode to reference mode -- the steady-state governance schema assumes full authority
post-migration-handoff-to-steady-state.versioned-lane-inheritance: versioned lane directories (e.g. Workorders_2_5/, Workorders_3_0/) that survived the migration as classified ancillary or bridge content must be declared in the steady-state governance vision or rules -- undeclared versioned lanes become unclassified surfaces and drift

schema.encoding: GOV2GOV-hello.md, GOV2GOV-source-target-map.md, GOV2GOV-rules.md, and GOV2GOV-pitfalls.md use flat labeled key-value markdown -- nested objects indented two spaces -- arrays as hyphenated bullet lists -- no frontmatter markers, no code fences -- field names must match schema declarations exactly -- GOV2GOV-registry.json uses JSON encoding

GOV2GOV-hello.md.context-ordering-rule: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- the direction of this relationship is critical
